title: User-defined types should not be passed as variadic arguments
description: Variadic arguments allow a function to accept any number of arguments (in this rule, we are not talking about variadic templates, but about functions with ellipses). But these arguments have to respect some criteria to be handled properly. The standard imposes some requirements on the class types that can be passed as variadic arguments, and those requirements vary according to the C++ standard version in use: Before C++11, the standard only allows POD types to be used as variadic arguments.
recommendation: Replace with the recommended alternative approach.

checks.message: User-defined types should not be passed as variadic arguments. Replace with the recommended alternative approach.
